items.title: Remember When It Matters: Proactive Memory Agent for Long-Horizon Agents
items.summary: In long-horizon tasks, decision-relevant state is often scattered across an expanding trajectory, while the action agent must surface it and act. As trajectories grow, task requirements, environment facts, prior attempts, diagnoses, and open subgoals can be buried in the context

items.title: Qwen-Music Technical Report
items.summary: In this report, we introduce Qwen-Music, a powerful music generation model capable of producing highly musical and high-fidelity songs with complete vocal singing. Qwen-Music supports two core tasks: Text to Music Generation, which create entirely new songs from text descriptions

items.title: Mach-Mind-4-Flash Technical Report
items.summary: We present Mach-Mind-4-Flash, a 35B-parameter Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) agentic model with 3B activated parameters. Through post-training optimization alone without scaling pre-training compute, the model achieves performance on par with or surpassing that of 100B-parameter-class

items.title: Robustifying Vision-Language Models via Test-Time Prompt Adaptation
items.summary: Pre-trained Vision-Language Models (VLMs) such as CLIP achieve strong zero-shot generalization, but their performance degrades sharply under adversarial perturbations. Existing test-time adaptation methods typically rely on sample-level confidence heuristics, overlooking the intr

items.title: The Dollar’s Hidden Dependence on the AI Trade
items.summary: Net foreign inflows into US equities have surged to a record high, driven in large part by overseas investors seeking AI exposure they cannot get in their home markets, see chart below.

items.title: Jevons Paradox: More Evidence of a Positive Employment Effect of AI Adoption
items.summary: A new study from Ramp, linking observed AI spending to workforce records across 21,559 US firms, finds that companies adopting AI grow their headcount 10% over the two years following adoption.

items.summary: Hyperscaler debt issuance continues to grow, reshaping dynamics across financial markets.

items.summary: Since congestion pricing launched in January 2025, about 500,000 vehicles enter Manhattan's Congestion Relief Zone on a typical weekday.

items.title: Japan: Housing Demand Declining
items.summary: Japan's shrinking population has left 14% of its housing stock vacant, a share that has risen for decades and shows no sign of reversing, see chart below.

items.title: China's Real Estate Slump Enters Its Fifth Year
items.summary: China's home prices have now been falling for four straight years, with both new and used home prices stuck in negative territory since 2022, see chart below.

items.title: The Market Disagrees With How Software Loans Are Rated
items.summary: Current ratings for software loans cluster heavily in the B2 and B3 buckets, but market-implied ratings spread loans toward both ends, a sign the market sees more credit dispersion in software leveraged loans than agency ratings currently reflect.

items.title: Japan bonds tell global repricing story
items.summary: Japan offers a clear example of how shifting Federal Reserve expectations are reverberating worldwide. We favor income and stay selective in this environment.

items.title: Global insights

items.summary: Middle East military escalation puts energy flows and oil prices in focus. We see this as a volatility shock rather than a sustained supply shock supply chains and would lean against market overreactions.

items.title: U.S.-Venezuela: limited risk to global markets
items.summary: U.S. action in Venezuela underscores deeper geopolitical fragmentation but limited spillovers to global markets. We remain risk-on, overweight U.S. equities and EM hard-currency bonds.

items.title: Global insights

items.title: Weak-to-Strong Generalization via Direct On-Policy Distillation
items.summary: Direct On-Policy Distillation transfers reinforcement learning improvements from smaller to larger models by using the policy shift induced by RL as an implicit reward signal, enabling efficient scaling of training without re-running expensive RL on the target model.

items.title: Memory Chips and China, Microsoft and Chinese Models
items.summary: The big three memory makers may come to regret opening up the door to Chinese memory makers; Microsoft, meanwhile, is very incentivized to use Chinese models.

items.title: Apple Price Increases, Apple Intelligence and the E.U.
items.summary: Apple is (finally) raising prices, but they're not shipping Siri AI to the E.U.

items.title: Pinwheel launches a retro-inspired landline phone for kids
items.summary: Kid-friendly tech company Pinwheel announced the launch of a new landline phone designed to let children stay connected without the distractions of a smartphone.

items.title: Already rich, already successful, why the last wave of tech winners is grinding again
items.summary: They're rolling up their sleeves again, seemingly out of fear of missing AI's defining moment and, presumably, the irresistible allure of making even more money -- potentially a lot more.

items.title: Uber’s product chief on hotels, robotaxis, and why the company doesn’t want to be “everything for everyone”
items.summary: Uber Chief Product Officer Sachin Kansal walks TechCrunch through the company's financial-services ambitions, its increasingly complicated relationship with Waymo, its new AV Labs data operation, and how AI is starting to show up in ways riders and drivers will actually notice.

items.title: Video-generation startup PixVerse raises $439M, valuation soars past $2B
items.summary: With the cash, the company aims to expand its world model offering and reach customers across geographies.

items.title: X just tweaked its algorithm to make it more friendly, less battleground
items.summary: The social media site says it will amplify posts made by users' mutual followers' to give the feed more of a communal feel.

items.title: Hermes agent maker Nous Research in talks for new funding at $1.5B valuation
items.summary: The company is raising at least $75 million, led by Robot Ventures, with significant participation from USV and other prominent investors.

items.title: Satya Nadella has issued a shocking warning to companies using AI
items.summary: Of all the debates raging about the potential downsides of AI, there is one worry causing the most hand-wringing among AI enthusiasts in Silicon Valley — that the giant AI labs that sell proprietary models are somehow acting like Trojan horses.

items.title: Apple says former employee exploited ‘rare’ bug to download confidential files after leaving for OpenAI
items.summary: Apple would not comment on the "security breach," which allegedly allowed a former employee to download sensitive files from Apple's network long after he departed the company for rival OpenAI.

items.title: The wildest allegations in Apple’s trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI
items.summary: Apple’s trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI contains allegations that range from employees joking about unauthorized access to Apple’s systems to claims that job candidates were asked to bring Apple hardware to interviews. Here are the complaint’s most eye-catching claims.

items.title: Sam Altman’s space data center trash talk is what most experts already believe
items.summary: Responding to Musk accusing him of being a scammer, Altman said, "homeboy you're the one sellling [sic] public market investors on short-term space datacenters."
